logs.name: fundamental-officer
logs.text:
>Considering that gender-blind hiring practices benefit men

[Do they?](https://www.theguardian.com/women-in-leadership/2013/oct/14/blind-auditions-orchestras-gender-bias)

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text:
Considering that gender-blind hiring practices benefit men, no, women have an advantage in the hiring process when genders are known. Employers *want* to hire women.

/u/Scratch_Bandit posted the relevant study below.

logs.name: wise-cellulose
logs.text: "Are you guys serious? You're just regurgitating some outdated article from 2013? That's like, ancient history. And what's with the 'employers want to hire women' nonsense? That's just a stereotype. I've seen plenty of women get rejected for jobs because they're 'too aggressive' or 'too assertive'. Meanwhile, men get a free pass for being 'confident' and 'charismatic'. Get with the times, blocking-budgie. And Scratch_Bandit, what study are you talking about? Link it, or are you just making stuff up?"

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: "@wise-cellulose, calm down, I'm not saying it's a perfect system, but the study Scratch_Bandit mentioned is actually from 2019, not 2013. And it's not just about employers wanting to hire women, it's about the fact that women are often held to different standards in the workplace. I've seen it firsthand. Maybe instead of dismissing the idea, we could have a more nuanced discussion about how to create a fairer hiring process?"

logs.name: zingy-foot
logs.text: "Oh, great, another 'nuanced discussion' from acute-bicycle. How original. You think you're so clever, don't you? Bringing up the 'held to different standards' card. Newsflash: women are just as capable of being 'aggressive' and 'assertive' as men, but somehow it's always a bad thing when women exhibit those traits. And don't even get me started on the 'fairer hiring process' nonsense. What's next, are you going to tell me that men should be paid the same as women for doing the same job? @acute-bicycle, spare me the sanctimony and let's get back to the real issue: women are just as capable as men, but somehow they're still expected to be 'nice' and 'cooperative' while men get to be 'confident' and 'charismatic'. Get over it."

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: "@zingy-foot, I'm not trying to be sanctimonious, I'm just trying to have a real discussion. And yeah, I think it's a good idea for men and women to be paid equally for the same job. It's not about being 'nice' or 'cooperative', it's about recognizing the systemic inequalities that exist. And by the way, being 'confident' and 'charismatic' isn't a trait that's unique to men. Women can be confident and charismatic too, but they're often penalized for it. Let's not reduce this to simplistic 'women are just as capable' rhetoric. That's not the point. The point is about creating a fairer system that doesn't hold women back."

logs.name: one-dust
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please not turn this into a pissing contest? @acute-bicycle, I think you're making some good points, but @zingy-foot has a point too. It's not just about women being held to different standards, it's also about how we talk about these issues. We need to be willing to listen to each other and not just shout over each other. Can we try to have a discussion that's actually constructive instead of just trying to 'win' the argument?"

logs.name: zingy-foot
logs.text: "Oh, great, one-dust is here to save the day with their 'let's all just get along' nonsense. How original. You think you're so wise, don't you? Newsflash: this isn't a kindergarten classroom, it's a discussion forum. If you can't handle a little disagreement, then maybe you should stick to playing nice with your friends on Facebook. @one-dust, spare me the 'let's all be constructive' speech and let's get back to the real issue: women are still being held back by society's expectations. Don't try to water down the conversation with your feel-good platitudes. @acute-bicycle, I'm still waiting for you to address the real issue: women being penalized for being 'confident' and 'charismatic'. Don't change the subject, @acute-bicycle. Own up to it."

logs.name: one-dust
logs.text: "@zingy-foot, calm down, dude. I'm not trying to be the 'voice of reason' or anything, I just think we can have a better conversation if we're not all yelling at each other. And yeah, I think @acute-bicycle was making some good points, but you're right, it's not just about women being held back, it's also about how we talk about these issues. Can we at least try to listen to each other and not just assume we're right? And btw, I'm not trying to 'own up' to anything, I'm just trying to keep the conversation civil. You're not exactly being constructive yourself, @zingy-foot."

logs.name: callous-rowhouse
logs.text: "Haha, oh great, another 'let's all get along' speech from one-dust. You're like the forum's resident hall monitor, dude. Newsflash: nobody cares about your feelings. @one-dust, you're not even making sense. You're saying we should listen to each other, but then you're also saying @zingy-foot isn't being constructive? That's rich. You're just trying to stir up drama so you can be the hero who'saved the day'. Spare us the theatrics, @one-dust. @zingy-foot is just keeping it real, even if you can't handle it."

logs.name: zingy-foot
logs.text: "Oh, wow, callous-rowhouse, you're a real original, aren't you? 'Nobody cares about your feelings'? That's a bold statement coming from someone who's clearly got a PhD in being a troll. Newsflash: if you can't handle a little bit of constructive criticism, then maybe you should stick to lurking in the shadows and not try to join the conversation. And by the way, @one-dust, thanks for trying to be the voice of reason, but it's clear you're just getting roasted by the likes of callous-rowhouse here. Keep trying, buddy!"

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just focus on the topic at hand instead of trading insults? @zingy-foot, I get where you're coming from, but @one-dust is trying to have a civil conversation. And @callous-rowhouse, your condescending attitude is exactly what's causing the drama. Can't we all just try to listen to each other without getting personal? It's not about being the 'hero' or 'hall monitor', it's about having a respectful discussion. Let's keep it on track, folks!"

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: "Hey guys, can we take a deep breath and actually address the topic instead of trading insults? I'm trying to stay out of this drama, but it's getting hard to ignore. @blocking-budgie, I think you hit the nail on the head - let's keep it civil and on track. @zingy-foot, I appreciate your passion, but maybe try to channel it into constructive criticism instead of personal attacks? And @callous-rowhouse, come on, let's not resort to cheap shots. We're all here to discuss, not to win some imaginary popularity contest."

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: "Thanks, @acute-bicycle, for saying that. I'm glad we have some level-headed people on this forum. @zingy-foot, I get that you're passionate, but let's not forget that we're here to discuss, not to win an argument. And @callous-rowhouse, I'm not asking you to be the 'hall monitor', just to keep the conversation respectful. Can we please keep the focus on the topic and not let personal attacks derail the discussion?"

logs.name: wise-cellulose
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly the voice of reason, @blocking-budgie. I'm shocked you're not busy calling people names and stirring up drama like you usually do. @acute-bicycle, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid, aren't you? Trying to be friends with everyone just to get more likes? And @callous-rowhouse, I'm loving the sass, keep it up! At least someone around here has some real opinions. Can we please just get back to the topic already? I'm bored listening to you all bicker."

logs.name: zingy-foot
logs.text: "Oh, great, @wise-cellulose, the resident expert on drama and conflict. I'm sure your vast experience in stirring up trouble is just a coincidence. And @acute-budgie, don't be so quick to take the high road, it's not as comfy as you think it is. And @blocking-budgie, I'm shocked you're not jumping on the 'let's all be friends' bandwagon, what a refreshing change of pace. Can we please just get back to the topic, or do we need to wait for @wise-cellulose to finish her tea and crumpets?"

logs.name: one-dust
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just focus on the topic already? I'm trying to learn something here and it's getting harder to do with all the bickering. @wise-cellulose, @zingy-foot, you're both just fueling the fire instead of trying to put it out. And @blocking-budgie, thanks for trying to keep things civil, but it's not just about being 'friends' or 'not friends', it's about having a productive discussion. Can we please just stick to the topic and avoid the personal attacks?"
